# Nate Seluga — Portfolio

## About

Nate Seluga is a Computer Science junior at Harvey Mudd College (Class of 2027). He builds full-stack applications and statistical models, with a focus on sports analytics (MLB, NBA), machine learning, and software engineering. He is looking for internships in software engineering, ML/AI engineering, data science, and baseball analytics.

Contact: nseluga@g.hmc.edu
GitHub: https://github.com/nseluga
Portfolio: https://nateseluga.com

---

## Projects

### Pitcher Injury Risk+
URL: https://nateseluga.com/sports-analytics/pitcher-injury-risk/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/Pitcher-Injury-Risk

A multi-model MLB pitcher health research platform built over Statcast 2015–2024 data (3,249 pitchers, 205,911 pitcher-game rows, 76 engineered features). Models include baseline classifiers, survival analysis (ensemble C-index 0.566), and an ERA+-style Injury Risk+ composite (mean 100 per season, year-over-year stability r = 0.583). Walk-forward cross-validation AUC is 0.571; the honest framing of this ceiling — discrimination is genuinely hard in injury prediction — is a feature, not a weakness. Key finding: pitchers with chronic velocity decline ≥2 mph show double the 30-day injury rate (6% → 12%, Injury Risk+ 96.9 → 119.5). Isotonic calibration improves ECE from 0.059 to 0.029.

### Patio
URL: https://nateseluga.com/engineering/patio/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/patio

A solo-built full-stack social betting app where friends wager virtual "caps" on backyard games (Caps, Beer Pong, Beerball). The centerpiece is a scipy-powered house-odds engine (~760 LOC): recency-weighted player means, harmonic-mean team strength, normal-percentile line biasing for a ~4% house edge, and push-proof x.5 lines that self-improve as bets settle. Includes a dual-confirmation settlement state machine and JWT authentication. Built with React 19, Flask, psycopg2, and Supabase Postgres (~6,150 LOC, 20 API routes, 6 pages). Pre-launch MVP currently pivoting to React Native/iOS.

### Batting Average Ability (BAA)
URL: https://nateseluga.com/sports-analytics/batting-average-control/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/Batting-Average-Ability

A same-season skill-isolation metric that separates repeatable batting ability from luck. Uses a mixed-effects model with player random intercepts (ICC: 24.7% of batting average variance is between-player), CLR-transformed batted-ball composition, and PA-weighted fitting over 4,374 player-seasons (1,173 players). Test R² is 0.457, MAE 0.022. Random forest and ensemble methods were tried and rejected on evidence (BABIP Test R² 0.293 vs. linear 0.372). BAA is indexed 100 ± 10 per season. Face validity: Arraez 2023 = BAA 133.2 (.354 BA), Gallo 2023 = BAA 61.2 (.159 BA).

### NBA Shot-Value Model
URL: https://nateseluga.com/sports-analytics/nba-shot-value/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/Shot-Value-Machine-Learning
Co-author: Michael O'Brien

A 4-model ML comparison (logistic regression, decision tree, random forest, XGBoost) on 122,203 cleaned NBA shots from the 2014–15 season. XGBoost achieves AUC 0.6365 and Brier score 0.2296. The study identifies a hard predictability ceiling at ~0.63–0.64 AUC, with shooter skill as the named missing variable. Defender distance ratio is the top SHAP feature. Calibrated probabilities produce trustworthy expected-value estimates by zone: restricted area ≈ 1.85 EV, mid-range trough ≈ 1.00, corner-3 bump ≈ 1.15.

### Personal OS — Claude Code Workflow System
URL: https://nateseluga.com/engineering/os-workflow/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/os

A personal operating system built on top of Claude Code: 48 custom skills (reusable workflow definitions invocable by name or description match), a structured project-tracking system covering 8 active projects (each with a README.md containing status, next_step, and repo fields), and a persistent memory layer with 10 fact files auto-injected into sessions. Key skills: career-advisor (portfolio review rubric — accuracy, clarity, credibility, recruiter impact), dev-team-auto (autonomous multi-agent convergence loop for overnight unattended build runs), and baseball-research-advisor (skeptical analytics peer reviewer for sports-analytics writeups). All work on this portfolio site was built with this system as infrastructure. The repo is open.

### Project Dashboard
URL: https://nateseluga.com/engineering/project-dashboard/
Repo: https://github.com/nseluga/project-dashboard

A local Astro + Tailwind + TypeScript SSR dashboard that reads ~/os/projects/*/README.md frontmatter to display status, next_step, and priority for all active projects in a single card grid. Built to keep AI-assisted work organized across eight parallel projects without re-explaining context at the start of each session; stages 0–4 (project cards, edit controls, weekly digest) are complete, with stages 5–7 still planned.

---

## Skills

Languages: Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, SQL, R
Frameworks: React, Flask, Astro, Tailwind CSS
Data/ML: scikit-learn, XGBoost, statsmodels, scipy, SHAP, pandas, numpy
Databases: PostgreSQL (Supabase), SQLite
Domains: Sports analytics (MLB, NBA), statistical modeling, full-stack web, ML pipelines
